lab.c12biochemistry_bind_to_the_enzyme_s: bind to the enzyme's active site, competing with the substrate. This increases the apparent Michaelis constant (Km) but Vmax remains unchanged because high substrate concentrations can outcompete the inhibitor.
lab.c12biochemistry_bind_to_an_allosteri: bind to an allosteric site, altering the enzyme's structure. This decreases Vmax because functional enzymes are removed from the pool, but Km remains unchanged as the active site's affinity isn't affected.
lab.c12biochemistry_amino_acids_possess_: Amino acids possess an isoelectric point (pI) where they carry no net electrical charge. In a buffer solution, if pH < pI, the amino acid is protonated (positive) and migrates to the cathode (-). If pH > pI, it is deprotonated (negative) and migrates to the anode (+). At pH = pI, it remains stationary.

lab.c12organicsynthesis_thalidomide_poss: Thalidomide possesses a chiral center, existing as (R) and (S) enantiomers. Historically, it was administered as a racemic mixture, leading to tragic teratogenic effects caused by the (S)-isomer, while the (R)-isomer was therapeutic. Modern asymmetric synthesis employs chiral catalysts to direct the formation of a single desired enantiomer, achieving a high
lab.c12spectroscopychromatography_identi: Identifies hydrogen environments. Splitting (n+1 rule) indicates neighboring protons. Chemical shift (ppm) indicates shielding (e.g., closer to O is more deshielded/higher ppm).
lab.c12spectroscopychromatography_determ: Determines molecular weight (M+ peak) and structure via fragmentation. The base peak (tallest) represents the most stable fragment ion.
lab.c12spectroscopychromatography_separa: Separates compounds by polarity. A polar stationary phase (silica) holds back polar compounds. Using a more polar solvent increases the Rf (Retention factor) of all compounds.

lab.c6dietexercise_the_human_body_follow: The human body follows the law of conservation of energy. If expended energy is greater, the body burns stored fat to compensate.

lab.c9atomicstructure_group_7_elements_h: Group 7 elements (Halogens) become less reactive as you go down the group. A more reactive halogen will displace a less reactive halogen from an aqueous solution of its halide salt.
lab.c9atomicstructure_select_a_halogen_w: Select a Halogen water and a Halide salt solution. Mix them to see if a displacement reaction occurs and note the color changes.
lab.c9atomicstructure_unstable_heavy_nuc: Unstable, heavy nuclei can achieve stability by emitting an alpha particle (2 protons, 2 neutrons).
lab.c9atomicstructure_this_changes_the_a: This changes the atomic number by -2 and the mass number by -4, creating a new element entirely.
lab.c9atomicstructure_observe_a_sample_o: Observe a sample of Uranium-238 decaying into Thorium-234. Track the number of parent nuclei over time to understand half-life behavior.
lab.c9atomicstructure_in_displacement_re: In displacement reactions, a more reactive halogen can replace a less reactive one. Reactivity: F > Cl > Br > I.
lab.c9electrochemistry_this_redox_reacti: This redox reaction is exothermic. By tracking temperature changes as we add titrant, we can locate the equivalence point where temperature peaks.
lab.c9electrochemistry_rusting_is_an_ele: Rusting is an electrochemical redox process. Iron oxidizes to form hydrated iron(III) oxide.
lab.c9electrochemistry_subject_an_iron_n: Subject an iron nail to different conditions and observe the rate of rust formation over simulated days.
lab.c9environmentalchem_acids_react_with: Acids react with metals to produce a salt and
Acids react with metal carbonates to produce a salt, water, and
lab.c9organicchem_requires_uv_light_sunl: Requires UV light (Sunlight) to break Cl-Cl bonds.
lab.c9organicchem_explosive_reaction_for: Explosive reaction, forms Carbon and HCl.
lab.c9qualitativeanalysis_qualitative_an: Qualitative analysis is used to identify unknown ions or gases in a substance, rather than measuring how much is present.
lab.c9qualitativeanalysis_ignites_with_a: Ignites with a squeaky "pop" sound when exposed to a lit splint.
lab.c9qualitativeanalysis_relights_a_glo: Relights a glowing splint due to supporting combustion.
lab.c9qualitativeanalysis_reacts_with_li: Reacts with limewater (calcium hydroxide solution) to form a cloudy white precipitate of calcium carbonate.
lab.c9qualitativeanalysis_metal_ions_emi: Metal ions emit specific wavelengths of light when heated, producing characteristic flame colors:
lab.c9qualitativeanalysis_mix_chemicals_: Mix chemicals to build an equation...
lab.c9qualitativeanalysis_click_the_flam: Click the flame to insert the wire loop
lab.c9qualitativeanalysis_you_are_given_: You are given an unknown solid. When reacted with Hydrochloric Acid (HCl), it bubbles rapidly. The gas turns limewater cloudy. When the solid undergoes a flame test, a blue-green flame is observed.
lab.c9separationtech_mixtures_contain_di: Mixtures contain different substances that are not chemically bonded. We use various physical processes to separate them based on their properties.
lab.c9separationtech_used_to_separate_a_: Used to separate a liquid from a solution (e.g., pure water from salty water). The solution is heated until the liquid boils into a vapor. The vapor is then cooled in a condenser, turning back into a pure liquid (distillate).
lab.c9separationtech_used_to_separate_mi: Used to separate mixtures of soluble substances, such as colored dyes. As a solvent moves up the paper, it carries the dyes at different speeds depending on their solubility.
lab.c9separationtech_rf_distance_by_spot: Rf = Distance by spot / Distance by solvent. For the blue dye: spot moved 160mm when solvent moved 200mm.
lab.c9separationtech_calculate_the_rf_va: Calculate the Rf value for the Blue dye when the solvent front is at 200 mm.
lab.c9separationtech_why_is_the_condense: Why is the condenser in a distillation setup angled slightly downwards rather than upwards?
